<!--
Quality bar: every resource in this repo is TYPED, ANNOTATED, and load-bearing.
We cap each section at ~8 links, so a new one usually replaces a weaker one.
-->

## The resource

- **Title & link:**
- **Type:** 📄 paper · 📘 docs · 📰 article · 🎬 video · 🧑‍🏫 course · 🛠️ tool · 💻 repo
- **Stage it belongs to:** (1 Foundations · 2 LLM App Building · 3 RAG · 4 Agents · 5 Evals · 6 Product/Design/UX · 7 Shipping/Observability/Ops)
- **License** (for repos/tools): MIT / Apache-2.0 / CC-BY / other
- **Stars** (for repos, approx):

## Why it earns a slot

<!-- One or two sentences. What does it teach that the current links don't?
What senior/interview-relevant insight or hands-on skill does it unlock? -->

## Which existing link (if any) should it replace?

<!-- We keep sections tight. If nothing should be removed, say why the section
can afford one more. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] The link is current and not paywalled beyond a free tier
- [ ] I've noted the license (and stars) if it's a repo or tool
- [ ] The annotation says *why it matters*, not just *what it is*
